response:
Hello! Based on your issue, here are the most likely causes and solutions:

**If you are in a HackerRank Interview:**
It is possible that the tab you are looking for is currently locked. In the Interview platform, candidates see a blank screen or missing tabs until the interviewer unlocks them. Please ask your interviewer to click "Unlock" to make the tab visible to you.

**Regarding submissions not working:**
- **Submit vs. Run:** Ensure you are clicking the "Submit" button. If you only click "Run Code" or "Run Tests," the system will not record a final submission, and you may see a message stating "No code was submitted."
- **Timeouts:** Check if your code is being terminated due to a timeout, which can prevent a successful submission.
- **Processing Time:** If you are participating in a challenge, results may take a short time to process before they appear.

If you are not in an interview and are using the practice platform, please provide more details about the "apply tab" you are looking for, as this is not a standard feature of the practice environment.
justification: The user is reporting a missing tab and submission issues. Documentation on HackerRank Interviews explains that tabs are hidden until the interviewer clicks 'Unlock'. Documentation on assessment reports explains that users often run code without hitting 'Submit', leading to 'No code was submitted' errors. I have addressed both possibilities (Interview vs. Practice) based on the provided text.

response:
Hello! I'm sorry to hear you're experiencing a blocker with the Zoom connectivity check. Based on our documentation, this issue is typically related to network restrictions or browser compatibility.

To resolve this, please try the following steps:

1. **Check Network Allowlist:** If you are on a corporate network, VPN, or behind a firewall, ensure that the following domains are not blocked:
   - `*.zoom.us`
   - `*.*.zoom.us`
   - `zoom.us`

2. **Verify Browser Compatibility:** Ensure you are using the most up-to-date version of one of these supported browsers:
   - Google Chrome
   - Microsoft Edge
   - Mozilla Firefox

3. **Re-run the Compatibility Check:** You can verify your audio/video permissions and internet strength using the official compatibility tool here: https://www.hackerrank.com/compatibility

If you have already tried these steps and the error persists, please let us know.
justification: The user is failing the Zoom connectivity part of the HackerRank compatibility check. The documentation specifically lists required Zoom domains that must not be blocked and the supported browsers for this feature. I provided these specific requirements and the link to the compatibility check tool.

response:
To remove an interviewer from the platform, you must remove them from their assigned team. If a user is removed from the only team they belong to, their account will be automatically locked, revoking their access to the platform.

Please follow these steps:
1. Click your profile icon in the upper-right corner and select "Teams Management" from the drop-down menu.
2. Ensure you have Company Admin or Team Admin access, as this is required to manage team members.
3. If you are in the "Users" tab, look for the "Actions" column. The more options menu (three dots) located specifically under the "Actions" column is where administrative actions for that user are found.
4. Remove the interviewer from their assigned team(s) to revoke their access.
justification: The response is based on the documentation provided in 'Manage Team Members Prerequisite Managing team members' and 'locking-user-access-from-hackerrank.md'. It explains that removing a user from their only team locks the account and directs the user to the Teams Management section and the 'Actions' column, addressing why they might not see the option next to the name.

response:
Hello! Yes, you can update the name on your certificate. Please note that you can only update the name once per account, and this change will apply to all your certificates.

To update the name on your certificate, please follow these steps:
1. Open your certificate page.
2. In the Full Name field, enter the name you want to display.
3. Click Regenerate Certificate.
4. In the confirmation dialog, click Update Name.
justification: The response is based directly on the 'Certifications FAQs' documentation, which provides the specific steps and limitations for updating a name on a HackerRank certificate.

response:
To stop Claude from crawling your website, you need to modify the robots.txt file in your top-level directory. Please ensure you do this for every subdomain you wish to opt out from.

To block the bot from your entire website, add the following to your robots.txt file:

User-agent: ClaudeBot
Disallow: /

If you prefer to limit crawling activity rather than blocking it entirely, you can use the Crawl-delay extension:

User-agent: ClaudeBot
Crawl-delay: 1

Important considerations:
- Avoid blocking IP addresses, as this may prevent the bot from reading your robots.txt file and honoring your opt-out request.
- You can find a list of Anthropic crawler IP addresses at https://claude.com/crawling/bots.json.
- If you believe the bots are malfunctioning, please contact claudebot@anthropic.com from an email address associated with the domain you are reporting.

For more information, you can visit the Help Center at https://support.claude.com/en/collections/4078534-privacy-and-legal.
justification: The response provides the exact robots.txt directives needed to block or limit ClaudeBot, warns against IP blocking, provides the IP list URL, and gives the contact email for malfunctions, all based strictly on the provided documentation.

response:
To remove an employee from your HackerRank hiring account, you can manage their access through the Teams Management section:

1. Log in to your HackerRank for Work account.
2. Click your profile icon in the upper-right corner.
3. Select "Teams Management" from the drop-down menu.
4. Go to the "Users" tab.
5. Locate the employee you wish to remove and click the more options menu under "Actions."
6. Select the option to remove the user.

Please note that if a user belongs to only one team, removing them from that team will lock the user account. 

Additionally, if you need to remove multiple employees at once, you can use the bulk remove feature available in the Teams Management > Users tab. For more complete account management, you may also use the "deactivate user" option available under the enhanced administrative controls.
justification: The response provides step-by-step instructions on how to remove a user via Teams Management, mentions the effect of removing a user from their only team (locking), and notes the bulk removal and deactivation options as per the provided documentation.

response:
To set up a Claude LTI Developer Key in Canvas for your students, please follow these steps:

1. Sign in to Canvas as an administrator and navigate to Admin -> Developer Keys.
2. Click "+ Developer Key" and then select "+ LTI Key."
3. Enter the following details:
    - Key Name: Claude LTI
    - Description: Enter a short description for the Canva LTI 1.3 app
    - Redirect URIs: https://claude.ai/lti/launch
    - Title: Claude LTI
    - Target Link URI: https://claude.ai/lti/launch
    - OpenID Connect Initiation Url: https://claude.ai/api/lti/login
    - JWK method: https://claude.ai/api/lti/keys
4. Under Additional Settings, toggle Privacy Level to Public.
5. Under Placements, it is recommended to remove the defaults and add "Course Navigation" and "Assignment Edit."
6. Click "Save."
7. Toggle the state to On.
justification: The user is a professor wanting to set up a Claude LTI key. The provided documentation specifically outlines the steps to create a Claude LTI Developer Key in Canvas, which directly addresses the request.
